Loading simulation engine…

Match Simulation

Team vs Team · Cross-Era XI · What-If Replay

How it works: Pick two XIs, choose venue and who bats first, and get three-way match probabilities or a full bilateral series distribution. Ratings are era-normalised career averages — Bradman's 2.99 means he scored roughly three times his era's global batting average. This is the v1 baseline; a hierarchical Bayesian upgrade (v1.5) will replace these crude ratings once it beats v1 on held-out calibration.
Team A (home by default)
# Player Bat Bow

Pick both teams to begin
Cross-Era XI: Build a side from any players across history — Bradman and Bumrah in the same XI if you like. Click any slot to open the player picker, then search by name or filter by country. Ratings are era-normalised, so the crude v1 comparison is already accounting for the 1930s vs 2020s bowling-average difference.

Build both XIs to begin
How it works: Find a historical Test, start from the actual XIs and venue, then swap any player(s) to see how the match outcome probability shifts. v1 gives the match-level P(win/draw/loss) — individual innings simulation is a v1.5 feature.
Home Side (historical)

Method · v1

Ratings. A hybrid of career averages and strike rate, both era-normalised. Base: player's average divided by the weighted-mean decade-global average across their active decades (baseline 1.0 = era-average). Augmented: multiplied by (player's strike rate / era strike rate)0.3, so aggressive scorers get credit over pure compilers without overwhelming the underlying average. Higher is better for both batting and bowling; era-normalised so Bradman's 3.27 and Bumrah's 1.68 are comparable across decades.

XI selection. In Team vs Team, each side auto-picks the best eleven from its country in the chosen time slice — 6 top batters by batting rating + 4 top bowlers by bowling rating + 1 all-rounder, with filler if fewer than 11 qualify. Eligibility: "Current" = any player whose last Test was within the last 2–5 years; decade buckets = any player whose career overlapped the decade. Changing Team A's era syncs Team B until you change B independently.

Team aggregates. Batting Strength = position-weighted sum of the XI's batting ratings (top-order batters count ~3× more than the tail). Bowling Strength = sum of the top-5 bowling ratings in the XI.

Match outcome. Three-way logistic on D = (A_bat − B_bow) + (A_bow − B_bat), plus home effect and a bat-first effect (the decision made at the toss — which team chose to bat first). Fit on ~2,500 historical Tests. Out-of-the-box calibration: log-loss 0.97 (vs 1.10 uniform baseline), Brier 0.58 (vs 0.67).

Known v1 quirks. XI reconstruction drops ~3% of historical Tests where country-matching fails (e.g. ICC World XI). Empty slots default to a below-average filler (0.5 bat, 0 bow). All documented in sim_model.json.

Data files: simulations.html loads sim_ratings.json and sim_model.json from the same directory, plus calibration.json for the accuracy numbers above.
